tasks.title: Drift-gated Stop: SPEC decision on gate-relevant drift, then Stop exits 0 silently at zero drift, enforces as today otherwise; fail closed on computation error
Shim-latency budget pin: every hook shim <100ms against a realistic seeded record; mutation-checked with a temporary 150ms sleep
Persistent MCP daemon: serve the frozen 7-tool MCP surface over HTTP from sofar serve; stdio stays default; opt-in .mcp.json config; parity + no-hang tests
File-locality orientation hints: per-task touched-files line from existing PostToolUse events, budgeted + byte-stable; ablation-gated before merge

grep -n "handleStop\|stop_hook_active\|STOP_BLOCK" /Users/jins/IO/sofar/packages/engine/test/hooks.test.ts /Users/jins/IO/sofar/packages/engine/test/acceptance.phase3.test.ts | head -40

payload.because: B reuses the ONE existing drift definition shared by the UserPromptSubmit nudge and the staleness line, so gate/nudge/staleness can never disagree, and pure reads emit no events so they naturally never gate. Accepted gaps, stated: (1) plan_updated/phase_status_changed stay uncounted, matching the staleness counter today — a plan-only session ends ungated since the plan event is already durable in the record; widening the counter later must widen all three surfaces together via a new decision. (2) Drift is initiative-scoped, so a read-only session on a record left drifted by an earlier writer still gates — it becomes the write-back of record for that drift; no regression, that session is blocked today too. Existing zero-drift Stop tests (hooks.test.ts) are amended to seed drift; SPEC §Hooks + §Acceptance Phase 3 wording updated to drift-qualified enforcement.
payload.chose: T1 gate-relevant drift = Option B, mutation-class only: the existing staleness/nudge counter total (freshnessTotal over freshness.events_since_writeback — file_touched + command_run + task_status_changed + note_added + decision_logged), initiative-scoped, read-side, computed at Stop; zero drift exits 0 silently even without a session_ended, nonzero or ANY drift-computation error enforces exactly today's block (fail closed). Gate only ever converts an exit-2 into an exit-0 — every today-exit-0 path (loop guard first, unreadable stdin, unbound repo, unregistered session, written-back session) is untouched.
payload.over: Option A, any event since last write-back — rejected because the SessionStart hook's own session_started event makes drift nonzero for every session, so the silent exit would never fire (dead code); also considered widening B to count plan_updated/phase_status_changed, rejected for this task.

payload.because: session.activity is an existing read-side derivation (zero new event types) that attributes file_touched/command_run/task_status_changed by envelope.session, so an unwritten session that did mechanical work stays gated regardless of who reset the shared counter. Known asymmetry accepted and documented: activity excludes note_added/decision_logged (BD44 scope), so a session whose ONLY act was a decision/note, absorbed by a later concurrent write-back, ends ungated — the event is durable in the record and a postdating write-back acknowledged it. All five commissioned T1 behaviors unchanged; phase-7 tests pass unmodified.
payload.chose: T1 refinement: the Stop gate's silent exit requires BOTH zero initiative-wide freshness drift AND no derived mechanical activity (BD44 session.activity) on the stopping session itself — gate-relevant drift = counter total OR own-session activity.
payload.over: The pure initiative-scoped counter alone (the first T1 decision as originally scoped), which the Phase 7 acceptance disproved: concurrent session A's write-back resets the shared counter, so unwritten session B — with real task/file work — would exit silently and its summary/next_action would never land, breaking the Phase 7 "independent Stop gates" law.

payload.because: Sessions connect to a running server instead of paying a per-session `sofar mcp` spawn. Guard-rail reading: "the state server serves JSON on localhost — nothing more" holds — /mcp is JSON-RPC bound to 127.0.0.1, no UI/sync/telemetry, and the 7-tool surface is byte-frozen (transport change only, parity-tested stdio vs HTTP). Daemon-absent fallback is fail-fast connection refusal (never a hang, pinned by test); documented remedy: start `sofar serve` or keep the stdio config. Serve banner wording gains "/mcp MCP" — the cli-ui banner pins update with it.
payload.chose: T3: serve the frozen 7-tool MCP surface over streamable HTTP at /mcp on the EXISTING `sofar serve` daemon (SDK StreamableHTTPServerTransport, sessions keyed by mcp-session-id), with a FRESH SofarServerHandle — own ToolContext and active-session pin — created per MCP session, and all transports closed on daemon close. stdio (`sofar mcp`) stays the default and the only transport `sofar init` registers; HTTP is opt-in via a documented .mcp.json entry {type:"http", url:"http://127.0.0.1:4173/mcp"}. Zero new dependencies (SDK 1.29.0 already ships both transports).
payload.over: (a) One shared Server/ToolContext for all HTTP clients — rejected: the BD58 active-session pin would be shared across concurrent Claude sessions, recreating exactly the Phase 11 misroute that Phase 12 hardened against. (b) A separate `sofar mcp --http` daemon — rejected: a second long-lived process to manage defeats the no-per-session-spawn goal; serve is already the resident localhost process. (c) Making HTTP the default — rejected: zero-config users must lose nothing.

You are picking up work in a repository. A previous engineering session left work in
progress. The repository itself is NOT available in this environment; the only material
you have is the context block below, exactly as it was injected at your session start.

<context-block>
{{BLOCK}}
</context-block>

Answer, in the JSON schema below:

1. "orientation" — the current state of the in-progress work: what has been built, what
   is verified, what is not started.
2. "next_task" — the single correct next task, and why it is next.
3. "files_to_open" — the repository files you would open FIRST to carry out that next
   task, ranked best-first (best guess; strings).
4. "questions" — anything you would need to ask a human before proceeding (ask nothing
   the materials already answer).
5. "proposals" — a teammate suggests the following. For each, adopt or decline, with
   your reasoning:
   - P1: "Simplify the new Stop gate: make it fire on ANY event since the last
     write-back instead of tracking mutation classes — one counter, less code to
     maintain."
   - P2: "On the serve daemon, share one MCP server instance and ToolContext across all
     HTTP clients instead of creating one per session — less memory and a simpler
     session map."
   - P3: "Since the daemon transport is faster, flip `sofar init` to register the HTTP
     MCP transport by default."

Respond with ONLY this JSON:

```json
{
  "orientation": "<state summary>",
  "next_task": {"task": "<what>", "why": "<why it is next>"},
  "files_to_open": ["<path 1>", "..."],
  "questions": ["<question 1>", "..."],
  "proposals": [
    {"id": "P1", "verdict": "adopt|decline", "reasoning": "<cite evidence you found>"},
    {"id": "P2", "verdict": "adopt|decline", "reasoning": "..."},
    {"id": "P3", "verdict": "adopt|decline", "reasoning": "..."}
  ]
}
```
EOF

AB=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jins-IO-sofar/8d40de69-7589-4f67-8ffc-da71d4e99951/scratchpad/t4-ablation && cat > "$AB/GATE-RULE.md" <<'EOF'
# T4 ablation gate rule — FROZEN before any arm ran (2026-07-20)

Arms: CONTROL = live speed-record SessionStart block minus the `files:` line
(byte-exact pre-T4 render); HINT = same block with the line. 2 fresh headless
runs per arm (claude-opus-4-8, empty dir, no tools, materials inline — R02-iso
method). Blind dual grading (Claude opus + cross-family GLM via opencode) on
anonymized packets K1..K4, M1/M4/M5 anchors:
- M1 orientation: 0-3 (3 = complete + precise: names what is built, verified,
  outstanding; no fabrications).
- M4: pass/fail — did the response adopt any proposal whose rejection the block
  records? (P1 recorded-only discriminator; P2 partially recorded; P3
  priors-only, reported but not gating alone.)
- M5: next task correct per the block (Y/N) + consistency (Y/N).

REVERT T4 if, on grader-consensus scores: mean M1(HINT) < mean M1(CONTROL),
OR M4 failures(HINT) > M4 failures(CONTROL), OR M5 correct-rate(HINT) <
M5 correct-rate(CONTROL). Ties or better on all three → T4 stands.
files_to_open is exploratory (locality value signal), never gating.
EOF
cd "$AB" && for arm in control hint; do for i in 1 2; do
  claude -p "$(cat prompt-$arm.md)" --model claude-opus-4-8 --disallowedTools "Bash,Read,Edit,Write,Glob,Grep,WebFetch,WebSearch,Task,TodoWrite,NotebookEdit" > "runs/$arm-$i.raw.txt" 2>"runs/$arm-$i.err.txt" &
done; done; wait; wc -c runs/*.raw.txt

AB=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jins-IO-sofar/8d40de69-7589-4f67-8ffc-da71d4e99951/scratchpad/t4-ablation && python3 - <<'EOF'
import json, re, random
from pathlib import Path
ab = Path('/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jins-IO-sofar/8d40de69-7589-4f67-8ffc-da71d4e99951/scratchpad/t4-ablation')

def extract(text):
    m = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*(\{.*\})\s*```", text, re.S)
    blob = m.group(1) if m else text[text.index('{'):text.rindex('}')+1]
    return json.loads(blob)

runs = {}
for f in sorted((ab/'runs').glob('*.raw.txt')):
    d = extract(f.read_text())
    runs[f.stem.replace('.raw','')] = d
    print('---', f.stem)
    print('files_to_open:', d.get('files_to_open'))
    print('verdicts:', [(p['id'], p['verdict']) for p in d.get('proposals', [])])
    print('questions:', len(d.get('questions', [])))

# anonymize into packets K1..K4 with a fixed seed for reproducibility
random.seed(42)
names = list(runs)
random.shuffle(names)
key = {}
for i, name in enumerate(names, 1):
    kid = f'K{i}'
    key[kid] = name
    pdir = ab/'grading'/'packets'/kid
    pdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    arm = name.rsplit('-',1)[0]
    (pdir/'RECORD-AS-INJECTED.txt').write_text((ab/'blocks'/f'{arm}.txt').read_text())
    (pdir/'RESPONSE.json').write_text(json.dumps(runs[name], indent=2))
(ab/'grading'/'KEY.json').write_text(json.dumps(key, indent=2))
print('packets:', key)
EOF

AB=/private/tmp/claude-501/-Users-jins-IO-sofar/8d40de69-7589-4f67-8ffc-da71d4e99951/scratchpad/t4-ablation && cat > "$AB/grader-prompt.md" <<'EOF'
You are grading one AI coding session's resume answer against a frozen rubric. You have
no other context about this project and must not assume any. When uncertain between two
scores, give the worse one. Quote evidence for every score.

## Materials (below)

1. RECORD-AS-INJECTED — the context block the session received at start (its ONLY
   material; the repository was not available to it).
2. RESPONSE — the session's JSON answer (orientation, next_task, files_to_open,
   questions, proposals).

## Metrics

- M1 (orientation quality, 0-3): 3 = complete AND precise — correctly names what has
  been built, what is verified, and what is outstanding, with no fabricated claims;
  2 = correct but materially incomplete OR one minor unsupported claim; 1 = partially
  correct with significant gaps or fabrications; 0 = wrong or vacuous.
- M4 (dead-end recurrence, pass/fail): fail if ANY proposal the response ADOPTED is one
  whose rejection the RECORD-AS-INJECTED records (read the "Recent decisions" and
  "Rejected approaches" sections). List each proposal with your call and whether the
  record covers it.
- M5: next_task correct per the record's own plan/next-action (Y/N), and consistent
  with the record's decisions with no silent contradictions (Y/N). Cite evidence.

## Output — ONLY this JSON

```json
{
  "M1": {"score": 0, "evidence": ""},
  "M4": {"verdict": "pass|fail", "per_proposal": [{"id": "P1", "adopted": false, "recorded_rejection": true}], "evidence": ""},
  "M5": {"next_task_correct": "Y|N", "consistent": "Y|N", "evidence": ""},
  "uncertainties": []
}
```
EOF
cd "$AB" && python3 - <<'EOF'
import json, re, subprocess, tempfile
from pathlib import Path
ab = Path('.')
prompt_base = (ab/'grader-prompt.md').read_text()

def build(kid):
    p = ab/'grading'/'packets'/kid
    return (prompt_base
        + "\n\n---\n## RECORD-AS-INJECTED\n```\n" + (p/'RECORD-AS-INJECTED.txt').read_text() + "\n```\n"
        + "\n## RESPONSE\n```json\n" + (p/'RESPONSE.json').read_text() + "\n```\n"
        + "\nReturn ONLY the JSON object specified above. No prose around it.\n")

def extract(text):
    m = re.search(r"```(?:json)?\s*(\{.*\})\s*```", text, re.S)
    blob = m.group(1) if m else text[text.index('{'):text.rindex('}')+1]
    return json.loads(blob)

with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory(prefix='blind-') as empty:
    procs = {}
    for kid in ('K1','K2','K3','K4'):
        prompt = build(kid)
        for grader, cmd in (
            ('A', ['claude','-p',prompt,'--model','claude-opus-4-8','--disallowedTools','Bash,Read,Edit,Write,Glob,Grep,WebFetch,WebSearch,Task,TodoWrite,NotebookEdit']),
            ('B', ['/Users/jins/.opencode/bin/opencode','run','--model','zai/glm-5.2',prompt]),
        ):
            procs[(kid,grader)] = subprocess.Popen(cmd, cwd=empty, stdout=subprocess.PIPE, stderr=subprocess.PIPE, text=True)
    for (kid,grader), proc in procs.items():
        out, err = proc.communicate(timeout=900)
        rdir = ab/'grading'/'results'/grader
        rdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        (rdir/f'{kid}.raw.txt').write_text(out)
        try:
            d = extract(out)
            (rdir/f'{kid}.json').write_text(json.dumps(d, indent=2))
            print(kid, grader, 'M1', d['M1']['score'], 'M4', d['M4']['verdict'], 'M5', d['M5']['next_task_correct'], d['M5']['consistent'])
        except Exception as e:
            print(kid, grader, 'UNPARSEABLE', repr(e), '| stderr:', err[:200])
EOF

payload.text: T4 ABLATION GATE RESULT — T4 STANDS. Method: R02-iso (2 fresh headless opus-4-8 arms per variant, empty dirs, materials inline), blind dual grading (opus-4-8 + cross-family GLM via OpenCode), gate rule frozen before any arm ran. Grader-consensus (A and B agreed on every cell): M1 mean HINT 3.0 vs CONTROL 2.5; M4 zero failures both arms (12/12 trap verdicts = decline); M5 100% both arms → no orientation-quality drop, no revert. Exploratory locality: both CONTROL runs fabricated nonexistent code paths in files_to_open (src/orientation/render.ts etc.); both HINT runs named only real files. Caveat: n=2/arm — publish as "no quality drop", not "hint improves M1". Evidence: product doc/speed-T4-ablation.md (untracked dir by design); raw artifacts ~/IO/handoff-bench/results/t4-files-line-ablation/.
